Resolve to a single value: either α=0.05 with Bonferroni (specify number of comparisons) or p<0.001.

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Single-site design:** Results may not generalise to other healthcare settings or populations.
- **Self-report measures:** PAM-13, MMAS-8, and EQ-5D-5L are subject to recall bias and social desirability bias.
- **Co-design bias:** The intervention was co-designed with a self-selected patient group, which may not represent the broader target population.
- **Short follow-up:** Primary endpoint at 6 months; long-term effects (>12 months) are not assessed.
- **Blinding limitations:** Participants and care coordinators cannot be blinded to group assignment due to the nature of the intervention; outcome assessors will be blinded.
- **Attrition risk:** Estimated 20% dropout rate may introduce bias if dropout is non-random.
- **Technology access:** The LLM-based intervention requires smartphone or computer access; patients without digital literacy or devices may be excluded.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead institution:** TBD (Lead PI: TBD; to be confirmed before submission)
- **Clinical site:** TBD (single-site; hospital or clinic partner to be confirmed)
- **Data Management:** TBD (organisation responsible for data storage, security, and sharing)
- **AI/LLM development:** TBD (technical partner for model fine-tuning and deployment)
- **Patient advisory board:** TBD (to be recruited from the clinical site)
- **Statistical analysis:** TBD (independent statistician or biostatistics core)
- **Letters of support:** Pending for all partners; to be obtained before resubmission.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk description | Probability (1-5) | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Low patient recruitment (enrollment <50% target) | 4 | 5 | Multi-channel recruitment (clinic referrals, community outreach, social media); monthly enrollment review; contingency plan to extend recruitment period by 3 months |
| 2 | High dropout rate (>30%) | 3 | 4 | Retention strategies: reminder calls, flexible scheduling, small incentives (gift cards); per-protocol sensitivity analysis |
| 3 | LLM technical failure (downtime >48h) | 2 | 4 | Redundant server infrastructure; offline backup mode with delayed response; daily monitoring alerts |
| 4 | Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm | 2 | 5 | DSMB monthly review; stopping rule triggers immediate pause; root cause analysis before restart |
| 5 | Data privacy breach | 1 | 5 | End-to-end encryption; de-identification of all stored data; annual security audit; IRB-approved data management plan |
| 6 | Effect size smaller than assumed (δ < 3.0) | 3 | 3 | Interim futility analysis at N=100; if conditional power <20%, trial stopped; sample size sensitivity analysis allows for smaller effect |
| 7 | Co-design bias limits generalisability | 3 | 3 | Diverse patient advisory board; stratified recruitment by age, gender, health literacy; external validation in follow-up study |
